name: Okatsune 103 Bypass Pruning Shears
best_for: General garden pruning, flower cutting, light branch work
expert_note: The most recommended Japanese secateurs worldwide. Okatsune has been making these in Hiroshima since 1948. The high-carbon steel holds an incredible edge.

name: Okatsune 104 Bypass Pruning Shears (Large)
best_for: Gardeners with larger hands, thicker stems up to 20mm
expert_note: Same blade quality as the 103 but with longer handles for more leverage. Preferred by professional landscapers.

name: Okatsune 101 Bypass Pruning Shears (Compact)
best_for: Detailed pruning, flower arranging, small-handed gardeners
expert_note: The smallest in the Okatsune bypass range. Ideal for ikebana practitioners and precision work in tight spaces.

name: Okatsune 205 Hedge Shears
best_for: Hedge trimming, topiary, shrub shaping
expert_note: Japanese gardeners use these for niwaki (cloud pruning). The light weight means less fatigue during extended shaping sessions.

name: Okatsune 206 Camellia Shears
best_for: Picking flowers, deadheading, harvesting herbs
expert_note: Named for camellia picking but used across Japanese gardens for any delicate harvesting. The pointed tips reach into dense foliage.

name: Niwaki Hori Hori
best_for: Digging, weeding, cutting roots, planting bulbs
expert_note: The hori hori is the Swiss army knife of Japanese garden tools. Niwaki sources theirs from Sanjo, a city famous for blacksmithing since the 17th century.

name: Niwaki GR Pro Secateurs
best_for: All-purpose pruning, professional garden maintenance
expert_note: Made in Sakai, the same city renowned for Japanese kitchen knives. The GR Pro is Niwaki's flagship pruner with a hand-forged blade.

name: Niwaki Tripod Ladder 6ft
best_for: Pruning tall trees, niwaki shaping, orchard work
expert_note: The three-legged design is a Japanese garden standard. The single rear leg pushes into soft ground or between branches where a regular ladder cannot go.

name: Niwaki Mainichi Watering Can 6L
best_for: Seed trays, delicate seedlings, indoor watering
expert_note: The detachable copper rose produces a rain-like shower that will not disturb soil or seedlings. A daily-use tool in Japanese nurseries.

name: Chikamasa T-500SRF Bonsai Scissors
best_for: Bonsai leaf trimming, fine detail work, grape scissors
expert_note: Originally designed for grape harvesting in Japanese vineyards. The fluorine coating prevents sap from sticking. A favorite among bonsai beginners.

name: Chikamasa PS-8G Craft Scissors
best_for: Defoliation, deadheading, light bonsai trimming
expert_note: A workhorse in Japanese nurseries. The curved tip allows precise cuts flush to the branch without damaging surrounding growth.

name: Kaneshin Concave Branch Cutter 180mm
best_for: Removing branches flush to the trunk, bonsai shaping
expert_note: The concave cutter is the most essential bonsai tool. Kaneshin makes theirs in Seki, Japan's cutlery capital. The concave cut allows bark to heal over smoothly.

name: Kaneshin Wire Cutter 180mm
best_for: Removing bonsai training wire without damaging bark
expert_note: The rounded jaws slide under wire pressed into bark without scarring the tree. A must-have for anyone wiring bonsai regularly.

name: Kaneshin Jin Pliers 170mm
best_for: Creating jin and shari deadwood effects on bonsai
expert_note: Used to strip bark and create the weathered deadwood look prized in bonsai. Kaneshin jin pliers have the jaw strength to crush and peel without slipping.

name: Kaneshin Root Hook Single Prong
best_for: Untangling root balls during bonsai repotting
expert_note: The single-prong design works better than rakes for carefully separating individual roots without tearing. Used during every bonsai repotting session.

name: Kaneshin Knob Cutter 180mm
best_for: Removing branch stubs and knobs for smooth trunk lines
expert_note: Where the concave cutter handles branch removal, the knob cutter cleans up the leftover stub. The spherical bite carves out protruding tissue for a flat wound.

name: Masakuni 8001 Bonsai Scissors
best_for: Precision bonsai trimming by serious practitioners
expert_note: Masakuni is the oldest bonsai tool maker in Japan, operating since 1919. White steel (shirogami) takes the sharpest edge of any carbon steel. These are heirloom tools.

name: Masakuni 8003 Bonsai Concave Cutter
best_for: Exhibition-quality bonsai work, professional use
expert_note: The gold standard of concave cutters. Each tool is hand-forged and individually adjusted. Many professional bonsai artists consider Masakuni the only choice for competition trees.

name: Tobisho A-Type Secateurs
best_for: Professional pruning, orchard work, garden enthusiasts
expert_note: Each pair is hand-forged by a single blacksmith in Yamagata Prefecture. Blue steel (aogami) holds its edge longer than any other steel used in garden tools. The tsuchime (hammer) finish reduces friction.

name: Tobisho SR-1 Hand-Forged Secateurs
best_for: Heavy pruning, thick branches up to 25mm, professional landscaping
expert_note: Tobisho offers a resharpening service in Japan, extending the life of these tools indefinitely. The SR-1 handles thicker material than the A-Type while maintaining the same forging quality.

name: ARS HP-VS8Z Professional Pruner
best_for: High-volume pruning, orchard work, repetitive cutting tasks
expert_note: The rotating lower handle reduces hand strain by 30% during repetitive cuts. Standard equipment in Japanese fruit orchards where workers prune thousands of branches per day.

name: ARS 130DX Bud and Fruit Shears
best_for: Fruit thinning, bud removal, detailed plant work
expert_note: A staple in Japanese fruit farming. The narrow blades reach into clusters to thin individual buds without disturbing neighboring fruit.

name: Nishigaki Pro 200 Telescoping Loppers
best_for: High branch pruning from ground level, tall tree maintenance
expert_note: Made in Miki, the hardware capital of Japan. The compound action multiplies cutting force, allowing cuts up to 30mm diameter at full extension.

name: Silky Gomtaro 300mm Pruning Saw
best_for: Branch removal up to 150mm diameter, general tree work
expert_note: Silky saws from Ono City are considered the best pull-saws in the world. Japanese saws cut on the pull stroke, requiring less force and producing cleaner cuts than Western push-saws.

name: Silky Pocketboy 170mm Folding Saw
best_for: Portable pruning, camping, trail maintenance
expert_note: The Pocketboy punches above its size. Professional arborists carry one as a backup. The folding design locks securely in both open and closed positions.

name: Japanese Nejiri Gama Hand Weeder
best_for: Surface weeding between plants, gravel path maintenance
expert_note: The nejiri gama (twisted sickle) is used in every Japanese garden. The angled blade skims just below the soil surface, cutting weed roots without disturbing nearby plants.

name: Japanese Kama Sickle
best_for: Grass cutting, weed clearing, rice harvesting, ground cover management
expert_note: The kama has been used in Japanese agriculture for centuries. The curved blade hooks around plant stems for fast cutting. Still preferred over powered tools in many Japanese gardens.

name: Bonsai Aluminum Training Wire 1.5mm (500g)
best_for: Training bonsai branches into position, shaping young trees
expert_note: Aluminum wire is softer than copper and preferred for deciduous bonsai. 1.5mm is the most versatile gauge, suitable for small to medium branches.

name: Bonsai Drainage Mesh Sheets (10 pack)
best_for: Lining bonsai pot drainage holes before potting
expert_note: Essential for every bonsai repotting. The mesh keeps akadama and other granular soils in the pot while allowing water to drain freely.

name: Akadama Bonsai Soil (Hard) 14L
best_for: Primary bonsai growing medium, mixed with pumice and lava rock
expert_note: Akadama is volcanic clay mined in Tochigi Prefecture. Hard-grade breaks down slower than regular grade. The color shift from dark brown (wet) to pale tan (dry) serves as a built-in watering indicator.

name: Kanuma Acid Soil for Azaleas 14L
best_for: Satsuki azalea bonsai, blueberries, camellias, acid-loving plants
expert_note: Kanuma is a yellow pumice from the same region as akadama. Its natural acidity makes it essential for satsuki azalea bonsai, which are a cornerstone of Japanese bonsai culture.

name: Tokoname Bonsai Pot Round Unglazed 20cm
best_for: Displaying finished bonsai, coniferous species
expert_note: Tokoname is the traditional center of Japanese bonsai pot production. Unglazed pots are preferred for conifers and masculine-style trees. The porous clay helps regulate soil moisture.

name: Tokoname Bonsai Pot Oval Glazed Blue 25cm
best_for: Flowering bonsai, deciduous trees, feminine-style presentations
expert_note: Glazed pots are traditionally paired with flowering or fruiting bonsai. The blue glaze is one of the most popular Tokoname colors, complementing autumn leaf colors beautifully.

name: Japanese Bamboo Rake (Kumade) 30cm
best_for: Raking leaves from moss gardens, zen garden maintenance
expert_note: Bamboo rakes are essential in Japanese garden maintenance. The flexible tines sweep debris without damaging delicate moss, which is treated as a prized groundcover in Japanese gardens.

name: Haws Bonsai Watering Can 700ml
best_for: Watering individual bonsai, seed starting, indoor plants
expert_note: While not Japanese, Haws watering cans are the standard at bonsai exhibitions worldwide. The fine brass rose produces a gentle shower that will not disturb surface soil or moss.
